items.title: LLM-as-judge disagrees with itself between runs
items.description:
The flap

I had a faithfulness gate on merge: judge scores every case, the mean has to clear 0.80. One Tuesday it failed at 0.79. I re-ran the identical job, no code change, no prompt change, and it passed at 0.82. Ran it a third time: 0.80 exactly. Nothing in the repo had moved. The judge

items.title: Designing B2B SaaS onboarding that converts
items.description: Most B2B SaaS churn happens before anyone ever cancels — it happens in the first session, when a new user signs up, stares at an empty screen, and quietly leaves. Onboarding is where signups become customers or become nothing. It deserves as much design attention as your core product, because it

items.title: Next.js vs React in 2026
items.description: The most common mistake in this debate is treating Next.js and React as competitors. They aren't. React is a UI library for building components. items.title: translateZ()
items.description: The translateZ() function moves an element closer to or farther from the user. translateZ() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateY()
items.description: The translateY() function shifts an element vertically by the specified amount. translateY() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateX()
